title: After death of 11-year-old Lyhanna, French public invited to courthouse voice anger at justice system
subhed: A courthouse in the Paris region opened its doors to the public on Wednesday after the fallout over the death of 11-year-old schoolgirl Lyhanna, which has raised questions about possible failings in the justice system. Around 60 people gathered to learn more and to share their views.

title: Vance postpones trip to Switzerland for Iran talks
Ukrainian drone assault on Moscow undermines Putin's promises of victory
In Trump's polarized America, empathy faces unanimous opposition
subhed: Once valued in American public life, empathy now faces criticism from the far right, which sees it as a sign of weakness. The left, too, is questioning its relevance: In a world where brutality prevails, can we still afford to show understanding?

title: As another heatwave hits, French government tries not to appear overwhelmed by climate crisis
subhed: According to the authorities, most measures in the national climate change adaptation plan are underway. However, critics say the plan lacks structure and adequate resources, as temperatures in France are expected to reach up to 40°C in the coming days.

title: Mont-Saint-Michel will no longer be managed by France's National Monuments Center
subhed: Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site, Mont-Saint-Michel will now be managed exclusively by the public industrial and commercial establishment (EPIC). The iconic Norman landmark was in a months-long standoff between the two operators, EPIC and the National Monuments Center (CMN), which had been jointly managing the site.

title: 'In France, 50% of buildings are not adapted to heatwaves'
subhed: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Hans, a specialist in building quality, said that around 10 million buildings in France are not adapted to high temperatures, even in cases where they were recently renovated.

'Incidents multiply in the Baltic Sea, where the Russian navy 'now operates in a completely overt manner'
subhed: After a Russian frigate fired warning shots at a civilian yacht in the English Channel on June 16, Rear Admiral Johan Norlén, chief of staff of the Swedish navy, describes an increasingly tense climate in Baltic waters.

title: French security replaces Palantir with domestic firm ChapsVision, a complicated choice
subhed: Used since the 2015 terrorist attacks, the controversial American company's data analysis software is set to be gradually replaced by that of a French provider. The long-awaited decision presents a significant technological challenge.

title: Trump welcomed at Versailles after G7: 'Macron has turned the château into a diplomatic extension of the Elysée'
subhed: In an interview, historian Fabien Oppermann said that the dinner planned for Wednesday highlights the French president's desire 'to impress his counterpart,' in hopes of preserving the transatlantic relationship at a time when ties with the US are especially strained.
